axiom Building : Type
axiom Amt : Type

axiom Managed : Building → Prop
axiom Rent : Building → Amt
axiom Deposit : Building → Amt
axiom More : Amt → Amt → Prop

axiom OliveGarden : Building
axiom twoK : Amt
axiom fifteenHundred : Amt

-- Context axioms
axiom OG_managed : Managed OliveGarden
axiom rent_OG : Rent OliveGarden = twoK
axiom more_2000_1500 : More twoK fifteenHundred

-- The security deposit at a managed building is equal to the monthly rent or more
axiom deposit_rule : ∀ b : Building, Managed b → (Deposit b = Rent b ∨ More (Deposit b) (Rent b))

-- Statement to prove: The security deposit at the Olive Garden is either $2000 or more.
theorem deposit_OG_2000_or_more : Deposit OliveGarden = twoK ∨ More (Deposit OliveGarden) twoK := by
  have h := deposit_rule OliveGarden OG_managed
  cases h with
  | inl hEq =>
      left
      simpa [rent_OG] using hEq
  | inr hMore =>
      right
      simpa [rent_OG] using hMore
</lean>

ANSWER: True

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom StrangerThings : obj
axiom BlackMirror : obj

axiom NetflixShow : obj → Prop
axiom Popular : obj → Prop
axiom Binge : obj → Prop
axiom Download : obj → Prop
axiom ShareWithLisa : obj → Prop

-- Context facts
axiom ST_pop : Popular StrangerThings
axiom ST_netflix : NetflixShow StrangerThings

axiom popular_implies_binge :
  ∀ s : obj, NetflixShow s → Popular s → Binge s

axiom binge_iff_download :
  ∀ s : obj, NetflixShow s → (Binge s ↔ Download s)

axiom not_download_bm : ¬ Download BlackMirror
axiom bm_netflix : NetflixShow BlackMirror

axiom binge_implies_share :
  ∀ s : obj, NetflixShow s → Binge s → ShareWithLisa s

-- From the biconditional we extract both directions
theorem binge_implies_download (s : obj) (hN : NetflixShow s) :
  Binge s → Download s :=
  fun hb =>
    (binge_iff_download s hN).mp hb

theorem download_implies_binge (s : obj) (hN : NetflixShow s) :
  Download s → Binge s :=
  fun hd =>
    (binge_iff_download s hN).mpr hd

-- Derive that Black Mirror is not popular
theorem not_popular_bm : ¬ Popular BlackMirror :=
by
  intro hpop
  have hb : Binge BlackMirror :=
    popular_implies_binge BlackMirror bm_netflix hpop
  have hd : Download BlackMirror :=
    binge_implies_download BlackMirror bm_netflix hb
  exact not_download_bm hd

-- Hence, "Black Mirror is popular" does not follow from the premises.
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

axiom ST : obj
axiom BM : obj

axiom Popular : obj → Prop
axiom Netflix : obj → Prop
axiom Binge : obj → Prop
axiom Download : obj → Prop
axiom ShareLisa : obj → Prop

-- "Stranger Things" is a popular Netflix show.
axiom ST_pop : Popular ST
axiom ST_net : Netflix ST

-- If a Netflix show is popular, Karen will binge-watch it.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Netflix x → Popular x → Binge x

-- If and only if Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will download it.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, Netflix x → (Binge x ↔ Download x)

-- Karen does not download "Black Mirror." And "Black Mirror" is a Netflix show.
axiom BM_not_dl : ¬ Download BM
axiom BM_net : Netflix BM

-- If Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will share it with Lisa.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Netflix x → Binge x → ShareLisa x

-- Goal: Karen will share "Stranger Things" with Lisa.
theorem share_ST : ShareLisa ST := by
  have b : Binge ST := R1 ST ST_net ST_pop
  exact R3 ST ST_net b
</lean>

ANSWER: True

axiom GymDaily : obj → Prop

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, EatSalads x → VeryConscious x
-- All people who grew up in health-conscious childhood homes eat salads regularly.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, HealthHome x → EatSalads x
-- All people who fulfill their daily nutritional intakes grew up in health-conscious childhood homes.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Fulfill x → HealthHome x
-- All people who disregard their physical well-being are not very conscious about their health and eating habits.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Disregard x → ¬ VeryConscious x
-- If people visit the gym at least once a day, then they always fulfill their daily nutritional intakes.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, GymDaily x → Fulfill x
-- Taylor either grew up in a health-conscious childhood home and disregards her physical well-being, or she did neither.

theorem taylor_not_gym : ¬ GymDaily Taylor := by
  -- Show the first disjunct leads to a contradiction
  have hnotAB : ¬ (HealthHome Taylor ∧ Disregard Taylor) := by
    intro hab
    have hA : HealthHome Taylor := hab.left
    have hB : Disregard Taylor := hab.right
    have e : EatSalads Taylor := A2 Taylor hA
    have v : VeryConscious Taylor := A1 Taylor e
    have nv : ¬ VeryConscious Taylor := A4 Taylor hB
    exact nv v
  -- Hence, from the disjunction we obtain the second conjunct
  have hneither : ¬ HealthHome Taylor ∧ ¬ Disregard Taylor := by
    cases A6 with
    | inl hab =>
        exact False.elim (hnotAB hab)
    | inr h =>
        exact h
  have nhA : ¬ HealthHome Taylor := hneither.left
  -- From ¬HealthHome and (Fulfill → HealthHome), we get ¬Fulfill
  have nFul : ¬ Fulfill Taylor := by
    intro hFul
    exact nhA (A3 Taylor hFul)
  -- From (GymDaily → Fulfill) and ¬Fulfill, we get ¬GymDaily
  intro hGym
  exact nFul (A5 Taylor hGym)
</lean>

  -- Hence, from the disjunction we obtain the second conjunct

    | inr h =>

  have nhA : ¬ HealthHome Taylor := hneither.left

  have nFul : ¬ Fulfill Taylor := by

    exact nhA (A3 Taylor hFul)
  -- From (GymDaily → Fulfill) and ¬Fulfill, we get ¬GymDaily
  intro hGym
  exact nFul (A5 Taylor hGym)

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Picuris : obj
axiom Harding : obj
axiom Juan : obj

axiom MountainRange : obj → Prop
axiom InNewMexico : obj → Prop
axiom InTexas : obj → Prop
axiom Visited : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Mine : obj → Prop
axiom LocatedIn : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Donated : obj → Prop

-- Picuris is a mountain range, and it is either in New Mexico or in Texas
axiom MR_Picuris : MountainRange Picuris
axiom NM_or_TX_Picuris : InNewMexico Picuris ∨ InTexas Picuris

-- Juan de Onate visited the Picuris Mountains
axiom JuanVisitedPicuris : Visited Juan Picuris

-- The Harding Pegmatite Mine is a mine located in the Picuris Mountains and was donated
axiom Harding_is_mine : Mine Harding
axiom Harding_in_Picuris : LocatedIn Harding Picuris
axiom Harding_donated : Donated Harding

-- There are no Texas mountain ranges that have donated mines located in them
axiom NoTxMRDonated :
  ∀ x : obj, MountainRange x → InTexas x → ¬ (∃ m : obj, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m x ∧ Donated m)

theorem Juan_visited_a_NM_mountain_range :
  ∃ r : obj, MountainRange r ∧ InNewMexico r ∧ Visited Juan r := by
  have ex_harding : ∃ m : obj, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m Picuris ∧ Donated m := by
    refine Exists.intro Harding ?_
    apply And.intro
    · exact Harding_is_mine
    · apply And.intro
      · exact Harding_in_Picuris
      · exact Harding_donated
  have notInTexas_Picuris : ¬ InTexas Picuris := by
    intro htx
    have hnone : ¬ (∃ m : obj, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m Picuris ∧ Donated m) :=
      NoTxMRDonated Picuris MR_Picuris htx
    exact hnone ex_harding
  have inNM_Picuris : InNewMexico Picuris := by
    cases NM_or_TX_Picuris with
    | inl hnm => exact hnm
    | inr htx => exact False.elim (notInTexas_Picuris htx)
  refine Exists.intro Picuris ?_
  apply And.intro
  · exact MR_Picuris
  · apply And.intro
    · exact inNM_Picuris
    · exact JuanVisitedPicuris
</lean>

ANSWER: True

axiom ClumsyFoodieExplorer : obj → Prop

-- If people spill a lot of food on their clothing, then they are not notably tidy.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Spill x → ¬ Tidy x
-- All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try spill a lot of food on their clothing.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ClumsyFoodieExplorer x → Spill x
-- If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → Tidy x
-- If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, ValueOS x → Cleanly x
-- All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPriorOS x → ValueOS x

-- Peter is either (spills ∧ cleanly) or (¬spills ∧ ¬cleanly)
axiom P0 : (Spill Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬ Spill Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter)

theorem peter_statement :
  (ClumsyFoodieExplorer Peter ∧ FamilyPriorOS Peter) ∨
  (¬ ClumsyFoodieExplorer Peter ∧ ¬ FamilyPriorOS Peter) := by
  -- From P0 and incompatibility of Spill and Cleanly, obtain ¬Spill ∧ ¬Cleanly for Peter
  have hnc_nc : (¬ Spill Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter) := by
    cases P0 with
    | inl hsc =>
        have ns : ¬ Tidy Peter := R1 Peter hsc.left
        have t  : Tidy Peter := R3 Peter hsc.right
        exact False.elim (ns t)
    | inr h => exact h
  have hns : ¬ Spill Peter := hnc_nc.left
  have hnc : ¬ Cleanly Peter := hnc_nc.right
  -- From ¬Spill and (ClumsyFoodieExplorer → Spill), get ¬ClumsyFoodieExplorer
  have h_not_cfe : ¬ ClumsyFoodieExplorer Peter := by
    intro hcfe
    exact hns (R2 Peter hcfe)
  -- From ¬Cleanly and (FamilyPriorOS → ValueOS → Cleanly), get ¬FamilyPriorOS
  have h_not_fpos : ¬ FamilyPriorOS Peter := by
    intro hfpos
    have vos : ValueOS Peter := R5 Peter hfpos
    have cl : Cleanly Peter := R4 Peter vos
    exact hnc cl
  exact Or.inr (And.intro h_not_cfe h_not_fpos)
</lean>

-- All female tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Iga Świątek.

-- All tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 are either female or male.

  have lostIga : LostToIgaRG Coco := A3 Coco fem

axiom Adores : obj → Prop

axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop
axiom DesignByMax : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, ZahaStyle x ∧ Adores x → Interesting x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Brutalist x ∧ Adores x → ¬ Interesting x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Adores x → ZahaStyle x ∨ KellyStyle x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, KellyStyle x ∧ Adores x → Evocative x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, KellyStyle x ∧ Adores x → Dreamy x
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

-- Derived: any design by Max that he adores cannot be ZahaStyle
lemma adored_design_not_zaha : ∀ x : obj, Adores x ∧ DesignByMax x → ¬ ZahaStyle x :=
by
  intro x hx
  have hA : Adores x := And.left hx
  have hD : DesignByMax x := And.right hx
  intro hZ
  have hI : Interesting x := P1 x (And.intro hZ hA)
  have hBE : Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x := P6 x (And.intro hD (And.intro hA hI))
  have hB : Brutalist x := And.left hBE
  have hNotI : ¬ Interesting x := P2 x (And.intro hB hA)
  exact hNotI hI

-- Thus, any design by Max that he adores must be KellyStyle
lemma adored_design_is_kelly : ∀ x : obj, Adores x ∧ DesignByMax x → KellyStyle x :=
by
  intro x hx
  have hA : Adores x := And.left hx
  have hZorK : ZahaStyle x ∨ KellyStyle x := P3 x hA
  have hNotZ : ¬ ZahaStyle x := adored_design_not_zaha x hx
  exact Or.elim hZorK (fun hZ => False.elim (hNotZ hZ)) (fun hK => hK)

-- Hence, any design by Max that he adores is evocative and dreamy
lemma adored_design_evocative_dreamy : ∀ x : obj, Adores x ∧ DesignByMax x → Evocative x ∧ Dreamy x :=
by
  intro x hx
  have hA : Adores x := And.left hx
  have hK : KellyStyle x := adored_design_is_kelly x hx
  have hE : Evocative x := P4 x (And.intro hK hA)
  have hD : Dreamy x := P5 x (And.intro hK hA)
  exact And.intro hE hD

-- Target would be: ∃ x, DesignByMax x ∧ Evocative x ∧ Dreamy x
-- but there is no existence premise from which to produce such an x.
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom Coco : obj

axiom RankedWTA : obj → Prop
axiom MostActive : obj → Prop
axiom LostToIga : obj → Prop
axiom FemaleRG2022 : obj → Prop
axiom MaleRG2022 : obj → Prop
axiom LostToRafa : obj → Prop
axiom AtRG2022 : obj → Prop

-- If someone is ranked highly by the WTA, then they are one of the most active players in major tennis.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, RankedWTA x → MostActive x
-- Everyone who lost to Iga at Roland Garros 2022 is ranked highly by the WTA.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, LostToIga x → RankedWTA x
-- All female tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Iga.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, FemaleRG2022 x → LostToIga x
-- All tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 are either female or male.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, AtRG2022 x → (FemaleRG2022 x ∨ MaleRG2022 x)
-- All male tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Rafael Nadal.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, MaleRG2022 x → LostToRafa x
-- If Coco is ranked highly by the WTA or lost to Rafael Nadal, then Coco is not a male tennis player at RG2022.
axiom A6 : (RankedWTA Coco ∨ LostToRafa Coco) → ¬ (MaleRG2022 Coco)
-- Coco Gauff is at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom A7 : AtRG2022 Coco

-- From A5 and A6, Coco cannot be male.
theorem coco_not_male : ¬ MaleRG2022 Coco := by
  intro hm
  have hRafa : LostToRafa Coco := A5 Coco hm
  have hnm : ¬ MaleRG2022 Coco := A6 (Or.inr hRafa)
  exact hnm hm

-- Using the gender dichotomy at RG2022 and coco_not_male, Coco must be female.
theorem coco_female : FemaleRG2022 Coco := by
  have hdisj : FemaleRG2022 Coco ∨ MaleRG2022 Coco := A4 Coco A7
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hf => exact hf
  | inr hm =>
      have contra : False := by
        have hnm : ¬ MaleRG2022 Coco := coco_not_male
        exact hnm hm
      exact False.elim contra

-- Hence Coco lost to Iga at RG2022.
theorem coco_lost_iga : LostToIga Coco := by
  have hf : FemaleRG2022 Coco := coco_female
  exact A3 Coco hf

-- Hence Coco is ranked highly by the WTA.
theorem coco_ranked : RankedWTA Coco := by
  exact A2 Coco coco_lost_iga

-- Hence Coco is one of the most active players in major tennis.
theorem coco_most_active : MostActive Coco := by
  exact A1 Coco coco_ranked

-- Therefore, Coco both lost to Iga at RG2022 and is one of the most active players.
theorem coco_both : LostToIga Coco ∧ MostActive Coco := by
  exact And.intro coco_lost_iga coco_most_active

-- Consequently, we cannot prove ¬(LostToIga Coco ∧ MostActive Coco); in fact, the conjunction holds.
</lean>

-- Entities and predicates
axiom obj : Type
axiom Man : obj → Prop
axiom Taller : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Block : obj → obj → Prop        -- Block x y means x can block y's shooting
axiom JumpWhenShooting : obj → Prop
axiom GreatShooter : obj → Prop

-- Specific individuals
axiom Michael : obj
axiom Peter : obj
axiom Windy : obj

-- Context axioms
-- 1) If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.
axiom R_tall_block : ∀ x y : obj, Man x → Man y → Taller x y → Block x y

-- 2) Michael is a man who is taller than everyone else in his class.
axiom Michael_man : Man Michael
axiom ClassmateOfMichael : obj → Prop
axiom Michael_taller_class : ∀ y : obj, ClassmateOfMichael y → Taller Michael y

-- 3) Transitivity of taller
axiom Taller_trans : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- 4) Peter is a man who is taller than Michael.
axiom Peter_man : Man Peter
axiom Peter_taller_Michael : Taller Peter Michael

-- 5) Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.
axiom Michael_blocks_if_not_jump : ∀ y : obj, ¬ JumpWhenShooting y → Block Michael y

-- 6) Michael cannot block Windy's shooting.
axiom Michael_cannot_block_Windy : ¬ Block Michael Windy

-- 7) Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
-- We encode "can jump when shooting" as the property JumpWhenShooting.
axiom Jump_implies_great : ∀ x : obj, JumpWhenShooting x → GreatShooter x

open Classical

theorem Windy_is_great_shooter : GreatShooter Windy := by
  -- From Michael cannot block Windy and Michael blocks all non-jumpers,
  -- deduce that Windy jumps when shooting.
  have Windy_jumps : JumpWhenShooting Windy := by
    by_contra h_not_jump
    apply Michael_cannot_block_Windy
    exact Michael_blocks_if_not_jump Windy h_not_jump
  -- Then apply the rule that anyone who jumps when shooting is a great shooter.
  exact Jump_implies_great Windy Windy_jumps
</lean>

ANSWER: True

  have hPro : ProBasketball Yuri := And.right h
  exact not_pro_yuri hPro

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Jane : obj
axiom KiKi : obj

axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Barks : obj → Prop
axiom Dog : obj → Prop
axiom Jumps : obj → Prop
axiom Legs : obj → Prop
axiom Terricolous : obj → Prop
axiom Likes : obj → obj → Prop

-- Jane does not like any animals that bark.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Barks x → ¬Likes Jane x
-- All dogs are animals that bark.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Dog x → Animal x ∧ Barks x
-- Jane likes all animals that jump.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Jumps x → Likes Jane x
-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Legs x → Jumps x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Terricolous x → Legs x
-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi
-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬Legs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi)

theorem kiki_not_bark_and_not_dog : ¬Barks KiKi ∧ ¬Dog KiKi := by
  classical
  -- First, show that KiKi jumps.
  have hJ : Jumps KiKi := by
    by_cases hL : Legs KiKi
    · exact A4 KiKi A6 hL
    ·
      have : Jumps KiKi := by
        by_contra hNJ
        have hTL : Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi := A7 ⟨hNJ, hL⟩
        cases hTL with
        | inl hT =>
            have hLeg : Legs KiKi := A5 KiKi A6 hT
            exact hL hLeg
        | inr hLeg => exact hL hLeg
      exact this
  -- Any jumping animal does not bark (else Like and ¬Like contradiction).
  have hNotBark : ¬Barks KiKi := by
    intro hB
    have hLike : Likes Jane KiKi := A3 KiKi A6 hJ
    have hNotLike : ¬Likes Jane KiKi := A1 KiKi A6 hB
    exact hNotLike hLike
  -- Therefore, KiKi is not a dog (since dogs bark).
  have hNotDog : ¬Dog KiKi := by
    intro hDog
    have hAB : Animal KiKi ∧ Barks KiKi := A2 KiKi hDog
    have hB : Barks KiKi := And.right hAB
    exact hNotBark hB
  exact And.intro hNotBark hNotDog
</lean>

ANSWER: True

<lean>
axiom Alg : Type
axiom Model : Type
axiom SOTA : Model

axiom IsML : Alg → Prop
axiom Supervised : Alg → Prop
axiom Unsupervised : Alg → Prop
axiom Reinforcement : Alg → Prop

axiom RequiresLabeledData : Alg → Prop
axiom UsedToTrain : Alg → Model → Prop

-- Any ML algorithm is either supervised, unsupervised, or reinforcement
axiom Cat : ∀ a : Alg, IsML a → (Supervised a ∨ Unsupervised a ∨ Reinforcement a)

-- Unsupervised algorithms do not require labeled data
axiom Unsupervised_no_labels : ∀ a : Alg, Unsupervised a → ¬ RequiresLabeledData a

-- There exists an ML algorithm used to train the SOTA model
axiom Exists_used_ml : ∃ a : Alg, IsML a ∧ UsedToTrain a SOTA

-- Reinforcement learning is not used to train the SOTA model
axiom No_RL_used : ∀ a : Alg, UsedToTrain a SOTA → ¬ Reinforcement a

-- The algorithm used to train the SOTA model requires labeled data
axiom Used_requires_labels : ∀ a : Alg, UsedToTrain a SOTA → RequiresLabeledData a

theorem supervised_used : ∃ a : Alg, Supervised a ∧ UsedToTrain a SOTA := by
  rcases Exists_used_ml with ⟨a, hML, hUsed⟩
  have hLabels : RequiresLabeledData a := Used_requires_labels a hUsed
  have hNotRL : ¬ Reinforcement a := No_RL_used a hUsed
  have hCat := Cat a hML
  cases hCat with
  | inl hSup =>
      exact ⟨a, And.intro hSup hUsed⟩
  | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hUnsup =>
          have : False := (Unsupervised_no_labels a hUnsup) hLabels
          exact False.elim this
      | inr hReinf =>
          have : False := hNotRL hReinf
          exact False.elim this
</lean>

ANSWER: True

    exact hneg hex

axiom NotPicky : obj → Prop
axiom SpendTimeDining : obj → Prop

-- If people at Mary's school work in student jobs on campus, then they need to earn money
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, AtSchool x → Work x → NeedMoney x
-- If people at Mary's school order takeout frequently in college, then they work in student jobs

-- If people at Mary's school enjoy the dining hall meals and recipes, then they spend a lot of their time in the dining halls
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, AtSchool x → EnjoyDiningHall x → SpendTimeDining x

-- Hannah is at Mary's school
axiom T6 : AtSchool Hannah
-- Hannah works; and if she needs money, then she is not picky and does not need money
axiom T7 : Work Hannah ∧ (NeedMoney Hannah → (NotPicky Hannah ∧ ¬ NeedMoney Hannah))

theorem conclude :
  AtSchool Hannah ∧ NotPicky Hannah ∧ SpendTimeDining Hannah := by
  have AtH : AtSchool Hannah := T6
  have hWorkAndImp : Work Hannah ∧ (NeedMoney Hannah → (NotPicky Hannah ∧ ¬ NeedMoney Hannah)) := T7
  have WorkH : Work Hannah := And.left hWorkAndImp
  have NeedH : NeedMoney Hannah := R1 Hannah AtH WorkH

  have hNP_and_notNeed : NotPicky Hannah ∧ ¬ NeedMoney Hannah := hImp NeedH
  have NotPickyH : NotPicky Hannah := And.left hNP_and_notNeed
  have NotNeedH : ¬ NeedMoney Hannah := And.right hNP_and_notNeed
  have contra : False := NotNeedH NeedH
  have SpendH : SpendTimeDining Hannah := False.elim contra
  exact And.intro AtH (And.intro NotPickyH SpendH)
</lean>

axiom Work : obj → Prop
axiom NeedMoney : obj → Prop
axiom OrderTakeout : obj → Prop
axiom EnjoyDiningHall : obj → Prop
axiom NotPicky : obj → Prop
axiom SpendTimeDining : obj → Prop

-- If people at Mary's school work in student jobs on campus, then they need to earn money
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, AtSchool x → Work x → NeedMoney x
-- If people at Mary's school order takeout frequently in college, then they work in student jobs
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, AtSchool x → OrderTakeout x → Work x
-- People at Mary's school order takeout frequently in college or enjoy the dining hall meals and recipes
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, AtSchool x → (OrderTakeout x ∨ EnjoyDiningHall x)
-- If people at Mary's school enjoy the dining hall meals and recipes, then they are not picky eaters
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, AtSchool x → EnjoyDiningHall x → NotPicky x
-- If people at Mary's school enjoy the dining hall meals and recipes, then they spend a lot of their time in the dining halls
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, AtSchool x → EnjoyDiningHall x → SpendTimeDining x

-- Hannah is at Mary's school
axiom T6 : AtSchool Hannah
-- Hannah works; and if she needs money, then she is not picky and does not need money
axiom T7 : Work Hannah ∧ (NeedMoney Hannah → (NotPicky Hannah ∧ ¬ NeedMoney Hannah))

theorem conclude :
  AtSchool Hannah ∧ NotPicky Hannah ∧ SpendTimeDining Hannah := by
  have AtH : AtSchool Hannah := T6
  have hWorkAndImp : Work Hannah ∧ (NeedMoney Hannah → (NotPicky Hannah ∧ ¬ NeedMoney Hannah)) := T7
  have WorkH : Work Hannah := And.left hWorkAndImp
  have NeedH : NeedMoney Hannah := R1 Hannah AtH WorkH
  have hImp := And.right hWorkAndImp
  have hNP_and_notNeed : NotPicky Hannah ∧ ¬ NeedMoney Hannah := hImp NeedH
  have NotPickyH : NotPicky Hannah := And.left hNP_and_notNeed
  have NotNeedH : ¬ NeedMoney Hannah := And.right hNP_and_notNeed
  have contra : False := NotNeedH NeedH
  have SpendH : SpendTimeDining Hannah := False.elim contra
  exact And.intro AtH (And.intro NotPickyH SpendH)

axiom obj : Type

axiom DriedThaiChilies : obj

axiom BakedSweet : obj → Prop
axiom Spicy : obj → Prop
axiom Cupcake : obj → Prop
axiom MalaHotpot : obj → Prop
axiom FromBBM : obj → Prop

-- No baked sweets are spicy.
axiom H1 : ∀ x : obj, BakedSweet x → ¬ Spicy x
-- All cupcakes are baked sweets.
axiom H2 : ∀ x : obj, Cupcake x → BakedSweet x
-- All mala hotpots are spicy.
axiom H3 : ∀ x : obj, MalaHotpot x → Spicy x
-- All products from Baked by Melissa are cupcakes.
axiom H4 : ∀ x : obj, FromBBM x → Cupcake x
-- Dried Thai chilies are spicy or mala hotpots or not baked sweets.
axiom H5 : Spicy DriedThaiChilies ∨ MalaHotpot DriedThaiChilies ∨ ¬ BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies

theorem dried_thai_chilies_not_fromBBM : ¬ FromBBM DriedThaiChilies := by
  intro hFrom
  have hCup : Cupcake DriedThaiChilies := H4 DriedThaiChilies hFrom
  have hBaked : BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies := H2 DriedThaiChilies hCup
  have hNotSpicy : ¬ Spicy DriedThaiChilies := H1 DriedThaiChilies hBaked
  have contra : False := by
    cases H5 with
    | inl hSpicy =>
        exact hNotSpicy hSpicy
    | inr hrest =>
        cases hrest with
        | inl hMala =>
            have hSpicy' : Spicy DriedThaiChilies := H3 DriedThaiChilies hMala
            exact hNotSpicy hSpicy'
        | inr hNotBaked =>
            exact hNotBaked hBaked
  exact contra
</lean>

ANSWER: True

theorem exists_animal : ∃ x : obj, Animal x := by

-- Some show airing at 8 pm on Monday gives out roses on TV.
axiom ExMondayRoses : ∃ s : obj, Show s ∧ Monday8pm s ∧ GivesRoses s

-- If a show gives out roses on TV, then the show is an episode of The Bachelor.
axiom RosesImpliesBachelor : ∀ s : obj, GivesRoses s → EpisodeOfBachelor s

-- The Bachelor portrays the lives of real people (modeled as: its episodes do).
axiom BachelorPortraysReal : ∀ s : obj, EpisodeOfBachelor s → PortraysRealPeople s

-- All shows portraying the lives of real people are reality TV shows.
axiom RealPeopleImpliesReality : ∀ s : obj, PortraysRealPeople s → RealityTV s

-- Breaking Bad facts
axiom BreakingBad : obj
axiom BB_is_show : Show BreakingBad
axiom BB_not_reality : ¬ RealityTV BreakingBad

-- Statement to evaluate: If roses are given out during Breaking Bad, then it is on Monday at 8 pm.
theorem breaking_bad_roses_implies_monday8 : GivesRoses BreakingBad → Monday8pm BreakingBad := by
  intro hRoses
  have hBachelor : EpisodeOfBachelor BreakingBad := RosesImpliesBachelor BreakingBad hRoses
  have hPortrays : PortraysRealPeople BreakingBad := BachelorPortraysReal BreakingBad hBachelor
  have hReality : RealityTV BreakingBad := RealPeopleImpliesReality BreakingBad hPortrays
  exact False.elim (BB_not_reality hReality)
</lean>

axiom dtc_info : Spicy DTC ∨ MalaHotpot DTC ∨ ¬ BakedSweet DTC

theorem dtc_neither_bbm_nor_baked : ¬ FromBBM DTC ∧ ¬ BakedSweet DTC :=
by
  have hb : ¬ BakedSweet DTC :=
    match dtc_info with
    | Or.inl hSp =>
        fun hbs => (no_baked_spicy DTC hbs) hSp
    | Or.inr (Or.inl hMala) =>
        let hSp : Spicy DTC := mala_spicy DTC hMala
        fun hbs => (no_baked_spicy DTC hbs) hSp

    by
      intro hfbm
      have hc : Cupcake DTC := bbm_cupcakes DTC hfbm
      have hbs : BakedSweet DTC := cupcakes_baked DTC hc
      exact hb hbs
  exact And.intro hnotfbm hb

axiom Listed : Restaurant → Prop
axiom ManyNeg : Restaurant → Prop
axiom RatingGT4 : Restaurant → Prop
axiom ProvideTakeout : Restaurant → Prop
axiom Popular : Restaurant → Prop

axiom A1 : ∀ x : Restaurant, Listed x → ¬ ManyNeg x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : Restaurant, RatingGT4 x → Listed x
axiom A3 : ∃ x : Restaurant, ¬ ProvideTakeout x ∧ ManyNeg x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : Restaurant, Popular x → RatingGT4 x
axiom A5 : RatingGT4 HPS ∨ Popular HPS

theorem stmt :
  (ProvideTakeout HPS ∧ ManyNeg HPS) → (RatingGT4 HPS ∧ ¬ ProvideTakeout HPS) := by
  have ratingHPS : RatingGT4 HPS := by
    cases A5 with
    | inl r => exact r
    | inr p => exact A4 HPS p
  have notManyHPS : ¬ ManyNeg HPS := by
    have listed : Listed HPS := A2 HPS ratingHPS
    exact A1 HPS listed
  intro h
  have mn : ManyNeg HPS := And.right h
  have contra : False := notManyHPS mn
  have notProv : ¬ ProvideTakeout HPS := False.elim contra
  exact And.intro ratingHPS notProv
</lean>

ANSWER: True

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Mixture : obj

axiom Hydrocarbon : obj → Prop
axiom OrganicCompound : obj → Prop
axiom ChemicalCompound : obj → Prop
axiom ContainsCarbon : obj → Prop
axiom ContainsOnlyOneElement : obj → Prop
axiom Alkane : obj → Prop

axiom AllHydrocarbonsAreOrganic : ∀ x : obj, Hydrocarbon x → OrganicCompound x
axiom AllAlkanesAreHydrocarbons : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → Hydrocarbon x
axiom AllOrganicsAreChemicals : ∀ x : obj, OrganicCompound x → ChemicalCompound x
axiom OrganicsContainCarbon : ∀ x : obj, OrganicCompound x → ContainsCarbon x
axiom NoChemicalIsSingleElement : ∀ x : obj, ChemicalCompound x → ¬ ContainsOnlyOneElement x
axiom Mixture_equiv :
  (ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture) ∨
  (¬ ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ¬ ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture)

theorem evaluate :
  (ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture ∨ ContainsCarbon Mixture) →
  (¬ ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ¬ Alkane Mixture) := by
  intro _
  have h_notCC_notCOE : ¬ ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ¬ ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture := by
    cases Mixture_equiv with
    | inl hboth =>
      cases hboth with
      | intro hCC hCOE =>
        have hNotCOE : ¬ ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture := NoChemicalIsSingleElement Mixture hCC
        have hFalse : False := hNotCOE hCOE
        exact False.elim hFalse
    | inr hneither =>
      exact hneither
  have hNotCC : ¬ ChemicalCompound Mixture := h_notCC_notCOE.left
  have hNotAlk : ¬ Alkane Mixture := by
    intro hAlk
    have hHydro : Hydrocarbon Mixture := AllAlkanesAreHydrocarbons Mixture hAlk
    have hOrg : OrganicCompound Mixture := AllHydrocarbonsAreOrganic Mixture hHydro
    have hChem : ChemicalCompound Mixture := AllOrganicsAreChemicals Mixture hOrg
    exact hNotCC hChem
  exact And.intro hNotCC hNotAlk
</lean>

ANSWER: True

      exact And.intro hS hN
    exact Or.inr hSk
